samples.raw_output:
```json
{
  "cefr_level": "B1",
  "grammar_score": "0.7",
  "corrections": [
    "Hi, i had a simular problem. -> Hi, I had a similar problem.",
    "this apps at Play Market -> these apps on the Play Market",
    "Don't spend a lot of time to TV -> Don't spend a lot of time on TV",
    "You should leave from useless groups -> You should leave useless groups",
    "It will bring you enought energy -> It will bring you enough energy",
    "For more information, you can write me at personal messages -> For more information, you can message me personally."
  ],
  "feedback": "The text demonstrates a good understanding of how to offer advice and provide tips. The vocabulary is appropriate for the task, and the sentences are generally clear. However, there are some grammatical errors (e.g., incorrect prepositions, verb forms, and spelling mistakes) that slightly detract from the overall quality. Focusing on prepositions and correct verb usage would be beneficial. The use of contractions ('I'd, you'll) would make the text sound more natural."
}
```

```json
{
  "cefr_level": "B2",
  "grammar_score": "0.75",
  "corrections": [
    "decition -> decision",
    "However, you shouldn't overload yourself so you need free time sometimes. -> However, you shouldn't overload yourself; you need free time sometimes.",
    "Firstly I want you to understand that there are different options how to organize your week. -> Firstly, I want you to understand that there are different ways to organize your week.",
    "each my day -> each day",
    "But in this case I don't have long periods of free time, I just have many breaks during a day and I rest within them. -> But in this case, I don't have long periods of free time; I just have many breaks during the day and rest during them.",
    "Also, though you shouldn't use other's works a lot, it's actually ok to ask your friends or maybe groupmates for some help. -> Also, while you shouldn't rely heavily on others' work, it's okay to ask your friends or groupmates for help.",
    "But very important thing: don't abuse this help. -> But it's very important not to abuse this help."
  ],
  "feedback": "The text demonstrates a good level of complexity and vocabulary, characteristic of a B2 level. The student attempts to provide helpful advice and uses some idiomatic expressions. However, there are several grammatical errors (e.g., incorrect word forms, subject-verb agreement, and sentence structure) and some awkward phrasing that slightly impede fluency. Addressing these errors would elevate the text to a C1 level. The overall structure and organization are logical, and the advice is relevant to the prompt."
}
```

```json
{
  "cefr_level": "B1",
  "grammar_score": "0.6",
  "corrections": [
```
```json
    "Somebody can solve it, somebody - not. -> Some people can solve it, others cannot.",
    "It is easily. -> It is easy.",
    "Now I want to some tips for became diffiecult study easilier. -> Now I want to give some tips to make studying easier.",
    "rewrite solution from other students. -> copy solutions from other students.",
    "do sport. It is not obviously tip, but it really work. -> do sport. It’s not an obvious tip, but it really works.",
    "your brain relax and do not do anything -> your brain relaxes and doesn't do anything",
    "spend less time for doing homework than before traning -> spend less time doing homework than before training",
    "More time of sleeping, more energy at work. It is obviously. -> More sleep means more energy at work. It’s obvious.",
    "If you sleep aproximately three hours, of course you do not have energy for working. -> If you sleep for approximately three hours, you won’t have energy to work.",
    "Do homework after getting. -> Get started on your homework.",
    "you dont spend time in future for doing this -> you won’t spend time in the future doing this",
    "making you eductation esalier -> making your education easier"
  ],
  "feedback": "The text demonstrates a good attempt at providing helpful advice, showing an independent level of language use. However, there are several grammatical and vocabulary errors that impede clarity. The sentence structure is often awkward, and word choices are sometimes inaccurate. Focus on improving grammatical accuracy (verb tenses, subject-verb agreement, prepositions) and expanding your vocabulary to express ideas more precisely. While the ideas are relevant and well-intentioned, refining the language will significantly enhance the overall quality of the writing. Consider using a grammar checker and dictionary to help identify and correct errors."
}
```

```json
{
    "cefr_level": "B1",
```
```json
    "grammar_score": "0.6",
    "corrections": [
        "You should not care that much about this. -> It's okay to feel overwhelmed, but don't worry too much.",
        "Everyone got same time a day -> Everyone has the same amount of time each day.",
        "To get more free time you should organise your whole week. -> To get more free time, try organizing your week.",
        "Write down all your deadlines to be done and all homeworks you want to start. -> Write down all your deadlines and homework assignments you want to start.",
        "Allocate time for tasks per day, and you'll see how fast you're getting everyday tasks done. -> Allocate time for each task each day, and you'll see how quickly you can complete them.",
        "Also Its important to understand what you actually need to do. -> It's also important to understand what you need to do.",
        "If subject seems not intresting and its not needed in next courses and for your life, why do you need to do all the tasks and get exhausted every day to get max grade? -> If a subject doesn't interest you and isn't necessary for your future courses or career, why exhaust yourself trying to get the highest grade?",
        "To sum up, you have to organise your days, set priorities. -> In conclusion, organize your days and set priorities."
    ],
    "feedback": "The text demonstrates a basic understanding of how to offer advice and provide tips. The vocabulary is relatively simple, but the sentence structure shows attempts at complexity. However, there are several grammatical errors (e.g., incorrect verb tenses, subject-verb agreement, and word choice) that occasionally hinder clarity. The text could benefit from more precise vocabulary and improved sentence construction to enhance fluency and accuracy. While the ideas are relevant, the expression could be more polished to reach a B2 level. Focus on refining grammar and expanding vocabulary related to time management and study skills."
}
```

```json
{
    "cefr_level": "B2",
    "grammar_score": "0.7",
    "corrections": [
        "I cannnot agree with this point.",
        "But there is different situation.",
        "Or maybe they want to work in science or in other difficult area.",
        "In the university people study different complex subjects.",
        "To sum it up, there is no one right answer to this question.",
        "The choice depends on the situation and needs of a particular person."
    ],
    "feedback": "The text demonstrates a good grasp of expressing opinions and supporting them with arguments, aligning with the B2 level. The vocabulary is adequate, and sentence structure is generally complex. However, there are several grammatical errors and awkward phrasing that slightly detract from the overall fluency. Specifically, pay attention to article usage ('a person'), subject-verb agreement ('people study'), and prepositions ('in science'). Also, consider rephrasing some sentences for clarity and naturalness. For example, 'But there is different situation' could be 'But the situation is different'. While the errors don't significantly impede understanding, addressing them would elevate the text to a more proficient level."
}
```
